name: Adroa
description: Adroa is a complex and multifaceted deity within Lugbara mythology of Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo. Adroa represents both life and death, good and evil, and the duality inherent in the human condition. They are often described as existing in the wilderness, particularly near unusual or dangerous places like large trees, termite mounds, or crossroads. Adroa are believed to be spirits of the dead who linger between the world of the living and the afterlife. They are typically invisible but can manifest to humans, often appearing as half a person, possessing only one eye, one arm, and one leg, symbolizing their incomplete state. This incompleteness also signifies their connection to the liminal space between life and death. Some Adroa are benevolent, offering guidance and protection to the living, particularly to their descendants. They can bring good fortune, health, and fertility. However, other Adroa are malevolent, causing illness, misfortune, and even death. These malevolent Adroa are often angered by disrespect or neglect and seek to punish those who offend them. Rituals, sacrifices, and offerings are performed to appease both the benevolent and malevolent Adroa, ensuring the well-being of the community. The Lugbara believe that proper burial rites and respect for the dead are crucial to prevent the deceased from becoming angry and vengeful Adroa. Adroa's influence extends to many aspects of Lugbara life, from agriculture and hunting to healing and social order. Their existence serves as a constant reminder of the interconnectedness of the living and the dead and the importance of maintaining harmony with the spiritual realm.
subtitle: Duality of Life and Death

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Adroa
keyMyths.description: This myth explains how the spirits of the dead become Adroa, lingering between the world of the living and the afterlife due to incomplete burial rites or unresolved issues. It highlights the importance of respecting the dead.

keyMyths.title: Adroa and the Hunter
keyMyths.description: A hunter encounters an Adroa in the wilderness. Depending on his actions and respect, the Adroa either grants him great hunting success or curses him with misfortune, illustrating Adroa's power.

keyMyths.title: Appeasing the Angry Adroa
keyMyths.description: This myth tells of a village plagued by illness and misfortune caused by an angered Adroa. The villagers consult a diviner and perform rituals to appease the spirit, restoring balance and health to the community.

sources.author: Middleton, John

sources.text: A comprehensive anthropological study of the Lugbara people and their beliefs.
